channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: No. 24 South Florida scores 4 TDs in a 3 1/2-minute span and hands North Texas its 1st loss
item.description: DENTON, Texas (AP) — Byrum Brown threw three touchdown passes and ran for two more scores, Jhalyn Shuler returned a fumble 34 yards for South Florida's fourth TD in span of 3 1/2 minutes and the No. 24 Bulls beat North Texas 63-36 on Friday night, handing the Mean Green their first loss this season.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polanco hits winning single in the 15th inning as the Mariners beat the Tigers to advance to ALCS
item.description: Jorge Polanco hit a game-ending single in the 15th inning, and the Seattle Mariners advanced to the AL Championship Series by outlasting the Detroit Tigers for a 3-2 victory in the longest winner-take-all postseason game in baseball history.
item.dc:creator: Luke Olson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Led by A'ja Wilson, the Las Vegas Aces have become a WNBA dynasty
item.description: A'ja Wilson and the Las Vegas Aces are in rarified air. The franchise won its third WNBA championship in four seasons, completing a sweep of Phoenix in the best-of-seven Finals on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tigers ace Tarik Skubal turns in a dominant performance in Game 5 loss to Mariners
item.description: Detroit Tigers ace Tarik Skubal turned in a dominant performance Friday night in the first six innings of Game 5 of the AL Division Series in a game that ended hours later with the Seattle Mariners winning 3-2 in 15 innings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wilson scores 31 and Aces win 3rd WNBA title in 4 seasons, beating Mercury 97-86 for 4-game sweep
item.description: A’ja Wilson scored 31 points, Chelsea Gray and Jackie Young both added 18 and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Phoenix Mercury 97-86 in Game 4 of the WNBA Finals, sweeping their way to a third championship in four seasons.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Albert Pujols has met with the Angels about their managerial opening, AP source says
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — Albert Pujols has met with the Los Angeles Angels about returning to their dugout as their next manager, a person with knowledge of the meeting told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota declines Trump admin's ultimatum to protect girls sports despite pleas from schools and athletes
item.description: Minnesota rejected the federal ultimatum over transgender athletes despite hundreds of school board members urging compliance with Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, Loyola Chicago's beloved chaplain, dies at 106
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, the chaplain for the men's basketball team at Loyola Chicago who became a beloved international celebrity during the school's fairy-tale run to the Final Four of the NCAA Tournament in 2018, has died, the university announced Thursday night. She was 106.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sister Jean, Loyola Chicago's beloved basketball chaplain and NCAA sensation, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, the beloved Loyola Chicago basketball chaplain who became famous during the team's 2018 Final Four run, has died at age 106.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Parity or poor football? NFL is providing plenty of close games with exciting finishes
item.description: Some call it parity. Others consider it bad football. No matter how it’s viewed, the NFL is providing plenty of close games, thrilling comebacks, big upsets and late-minute heroics. It keeps viewers entertained — or at least tuned in until the end.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ian Happ's homer sets the tone for the Cubs in a playoff victory against the Brewers
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — With one big swing, Ian Happ sent a charge through an electric crowd of 41,770 at Wrigley Field. After a rough start to the playoffs, it was a pretty sweet moment for the longest-tenured player with the Chicago Cubs.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matthew Boyd stars as the Cubs blank the Brewers 6-0 to send the NLDS to a deciding Game 5
item.description: Matthew Boyd pitched two-hit ball into the fifth inning, and the Chicago Cubs shut down the Milwaukee Brewers for a 6-0 victory Thursday night that pushed their NL Division Series all the way to a decisive Game 5.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Minnesota attorney general says trans athletes in girls sports 'doesn't harm anyone' despite complaints
item.description: Keith Ellison downplayed the impact of transgender athletes in girls sports after school board members urge policy changes to protect females students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voice of the Tampa Bay Buccaneers shares his favorite game call
item.description: If you're a Bucs fan, chances are Gene Deckerhoff has graced the airwaves as the play-by-play announcer for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers.
item.dc:creator: Will Richardson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dodgers reliever Tanner Scott removed from NLDS roster due to illness
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Reliever Tanner Scott has been removed from the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for the remainder of the NL Division Series because of illness. Scott was replaced by fellow left-handed reliever Justin Wrobleski.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Family ties fuel Grand Junction volleyball’s Ludlam sisters
item.description: The Ludlam sisters have been playing volleyball together for Grand Junction High School, and their lifelong bond has created a strong chemistry on the court that has been key to the team's success this season.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Bears' Tyrique Stevenson set to return to Washington year after embarrassing loss
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — Tyrique Stevenson says he’s ready to return to the scene of one of the most embarrassing moments in NFL history Monday night when the Chicago Bears visit Washington.
item.dc:creator: GENE CHAMBERLAIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James teased 'the decision of all decisions' announcement. It was an ad
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James had another “decision” to announce. Turns out, it was an ad. The Los Angeles Lakers star teased that he would have something to say Tuesday. The “decision” reference is a nod to how he announced in July 2010 that he was joining the Miami Heat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mistake-prone Chiefs blame themselves for crushing loss at Jaguars
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Kansas City won’t have to dig deep to figure out where things were awry in Jacksonville on Monday night. Patrick Mahomes had an interception returned 99 yards for a touchdown and the Chiefs were flagged 13 times for 109 yards in a 31-28 loss to the Jaguars.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez stabbed multiple times in altercation leading to charges against him
item.description: Former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez was pepper-sprayed and stabbed multiple times during a late-night altercation with a 69-year-old truck driver in a downtown Indianapolis alley, which resulted in criminal charges against the Fox Sports analyst, according to court records filed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MIKE MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL moves back above playoff line with 1-0 victory
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - In its final regular season home game of the year, Real Salt Lake came up with its biggest victory of the season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Noem: NFL ‘won’t be able to sleep at night’ over Bad Bunny Super Bowl performance
item.description: The NFL has faced blowback from conservative commentators and MAGA influencers over its decision to select Bad Bunny as the halftime headliner.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Week 7 Student Athlete Spotlight: Chase Harris, Carlsbad
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI) -- For the Prep Pigskin Report's Week 7 Student Athlete Spotlight, we recognize Chase Harris.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Week 7 Pigskin Idol Game: Clairemont 0 at Canyon Hills 28
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI) -- For the PPR's Week 7 Pigskin Idol Game, we take a look at Clairemont at Canyon Hills.
item.dc:creator: Sharisse Cohee, Fox 5
